note: CI eval gates (docs/eval-golden-set.md §6; decision 0011 ruling 6). Gates apply to AGGREGATE metrics. Ratchet UP only — lowering a gate requires a decision record. The corpus now holds 50 cases per language (100 total: en + hr) after the O7 launch-scaling pass (GAP-7), up from the 36-case corpus these floors were originally calibrated on. extraction_precision (spec 0.85) and verification_agreement (spec 0.90) sit at honest floors BELOW the run-to-run bands measured on that earlier 36-case corpus (precision 71.2–82.5%, verification 79.4–91.2% across five same-code runs — extractor variance, F2-B session log): a floor inside the noise band is a flaky gate, and a flaky gate gets ignored. A broken prompt lands far below these floors (the degraded drill measured 8.8%). The floors are deliberately NOT raised in this pass — the run-to-run bands on the expanded 100-case corpus must be re-measured on the live gate first (a threshold change needs a decision record); raise them once that variance is known. Never game them.
